message:
fix(delete): show full transitive cascade closure in dry-run preview

The delete command's preview mode (when neither --force nor --cascade is
passed) previously showed only first-level dependents, while the actual
--cascade path recursively deletes the full transitive dependent closure.
This mismatch meant operators could not trust the preview to show the
true blast radius of a cascade deletion.

Changes:
- Preview mode now calls `collect_sorted_cascade_dependents()` to compute
  the full transitive closure before rendering, ensuring the dry-run
  output matches what --cascade would actually delete.
- JSON/TOON preview: `cascade_delete` field is now populated with the
  full closure instead of an empty Vec, giving machine consumers an
  accurate picture.
- Rich-text panel: `render_dependents_warning_rich()` now takes both
  direct_dependents and full_cascade slices. Direct dependents are
  listed first with bullet markers, then any additional transitive
  dependents are shown separately with arrow markers and a count header.
- Plain-text output: Similarly shows direct dependents first, then
  lists transitive-only dependents with a count, and adds a summary
  line showing total cascade deletion count.
- Both render paths fetch issue metadata for all IDs (direct + transitive)
  so titles display for the full set.
- Added unit test `test_preview_cascade_closure_exceeds_direct_dependents`
  with a 4-node dependency chain (A->B->C->D) proving that the cascade
  closure is a strict superset of direct dependents.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
